🍺 Golden Ale
🏢 Saltdean Brewery
📝 Confession, this isn’t the bottle nor the day I drank this, however I needed something to represent this beer as I forgot to take a photo but I’ve been thinking about it. A lot. Another one from the Saltdean Brewery (of “Drunk Monk” #101 and ESB #108 fame), I drank their Golden Ale at the weekend. Now, I don’t know if this was situational bias (it was sunny and I’d just cooked an amazing BBQ for some wonderful friends) but this beer hit the spot. It was beautiful, well balanced and horrifically tasty. I could have sat and drunk it all afternoon. At 4.5% too, I could have done. Easily one of the best beers I’ve ever had. On top of all that, I was told today that it was the last one - so it gets a bonus exclusivity point.
🏅 10/10

📝 An interesting concept from @mikkellerbeer and @peoplelikeusdk. An IPA brewed with black pepper. First few sips were nothing special but once I got into the groove, and took a breather, the black pepper started to really come alive. Not overwhelming, but enough for you to notice in the aftertaste. Was spot on as an accompaniment to a pepperoni pizza. 👌 It's a tough one to score as I often rate beers based on taste and if I would buy it again. I'm glad I've had this, it was a nice taste but I can't say I would reach for it if I saw it on a shelf (except maybe for someone else)
🏅 7/10
#craftbeer #beerfluencer #craftbeerstagram #beerinstagram #craftipa #cheerstotheweekend #beerwegoagain #beer #ipa #fridayfeeling

🍺 Tea Party
🏢 People Like Us
📝 Another beer with a twist from @peoplelikeusdk. This time a cream ale with green tea. Nothing outstanding about this beer - good flavours but as a non-green tea drinker I can't say I picked up on any vibes. Smooth to drink and went down well.
🏅 6/10

🍺 Take Up Space
🏢 The Queer Brewing Project
📝 The sound of this raspberry and vanilla stout drew me into this, however I was disappointed in the lack of those flavours. I'm sure a more sophisticated palate would have discovered them, but for my simple tastes I found it lacking. Such a shame. Smooth stout otherwise

#stout #beer @queerbrewingproject @beer52hq #beerreview #beerfluencer #beerstagram

🍺 Bibble
🏢 The Wild Beer Company
📝 Now this. This is a @wildbeerco beer I can get behind. Lovely balance of flavour - refreshing and hoppy without being too overbearing. This is definitely one I would buy again if I saw it in the shop. Great way to end a somewhat interesting fling with Wild Beer
🏅 8/10

🍺 Dream Sequence
🏢 Yeastie Boys
📝 Excellent brewery name @yeastieboysaus! This American brown ale had notes of chocolate and orange. It was very smooth and went down well. Definitely an evening sipper. Might consider picking a couple of these up if I see them as an evening long drink.

#beerstagram #beersofinstagram #beer #ale #brownale

🍺 Craft Lager
🏢 Octopus Energy
📝 I won a crate of this beer in an @octopus_energy pub quiz. I've had a few Octopus Energy beers before, IPAs, and this is disappointing in comparison. Hardly any taste and fizzy. It's a good beer for when you fancy a drink but don't want to use up your good stuff

🍺 Origin
🏢 Northern Monk
📝 Have seen a few @northernmonk beers cropping up on my timeline so thought I would give them a go. I have had a cheeky couple already and I can tell you they are spot on. This gluten free beer is wonderful. Crisp, light and, surprisingly, remenicent of a wheat beer. Northern Monk are definitely on my list of "breweries to buy from again"

#beerweekend #beerporn #cannedbeer #beerstagram #beer #drinking #beerreview #review #aleporn

🍺 Unite Tribute IPA
🏢 Wild Card Brewery
📝 I drank this IPA without incident. Was ok, went down well and was a little bit disappointed when it finished. I wouldn't reach for it again but wouldn't turn it down if I was offered one
🏅 6/10

🍺 Future Proof
🏢 Brewdog Vs.
📝 @brewdogofficial Vs @moderntimesbeer. I appreciate the brewery above isn't "right" but wanted to group the Brewdog Vs. beers. Now about the beer... It's great to have a big beer again. It's only a few more ml (110 I think) but it makes a big difference in the longevity of a beer. This IPA was nice, but expensive. There are hints of fruit, it was smooth, had a lovely flavour and I could imagine drinking it on the beach or relaxing in a garden. 🏅 7/10
#ale #cannedbeer #ipa #beerporn #craftale #beerthoughts #review #thirstythursday #alereviewer #beerfluencer

🍺 Rapscallion
🏢 Northern Monk
📝 A @northernmonk beer Mrs Ale House picked up in Tesco for me the other day. Not one of their best but still better than a lot of other beers out there. Light and hoppy with a fizzy tingle at the back of the throat. Slightly fruity but nothing to write home about.
🏅 6/10
#cannedbeer #beer #aleporn #hailtotheale #review #beergasm #craftale #beerfluencer #beersofinstagram #beerstagram #ipa

🍺 Oats of Disobedience
🏢 People Like Us
📝 A creamy, oat stout from @peoplelikeusdk. Went well with a couple of squares of dark chocolate last night. I still don't think oat stouts are my thing but this was one of the better ones.
🏅 7/10

🍺 New World
🏢 Northern Monk
📝 Really loving @northernmonk at the moment. Absolute sterling lineup of beers. This one was classic craft IPA - hoppy and dry.
🏅 7/10

🍺 East Coast Pale Ale
🏢 Bear Island

🏅8/10
#alereviewer #bottledbeer #hailtotheale #beerthoughts #bottle #alereview #beerreview #bitter

🍺 Faith
🏢 Northern Monk
📝 Now this. This is the epitome of the craft beer. An absolutely amazing pale ale and one I would drink time and time again. This isn't my first one of these and it definitely won't be my last. Fruity, but not overbearing, refreshing and wonderfully balanced. One of the best beers I've had to come out of a 330ml can. Bravo @northernmonk

#beerreview #aleporn #beersofinstagram #beerfluencer #alereview #beerweekend #cannedbeer #hailtotheale #beesofinstagram #beerstagram #ale #faith #northernmonk

🍺 Blonde
🏢 Saltaire 
📝 This is very much a #latergram. This light, crisp beer was perfect on one of those hot summer days we had last week. Would very much have one of these again
🏅8/10

🍺 Investor IPA
🏢 Sori Brewing
📝 Another #latergram (need to get better at posting these when I drink them). Boring, classic IPA from my  @beer52hq box this week. Could drink a few of these and would definitely taste better if it was proper fridge cold. 
🏅6/10
@soribrewing

🍺 Kipling
🏢 Thornbridge
📝 This was such a forgettable beer. Tasted nice, but was not desirable in anyway. I drank it because it was there, not because I wanted more
🏅5/10

🍺 Bevvie Across the Mersey
🏢 Glen Affric Brewery
📝 Such a long title for a sub-par beer. Every few sips I would think it was turning a corner, but I would just be disappointed by the follow-up. You could smell the citrus/pineapple, but that was the only enjoyable thing about it. Drinkable, but avoidable. 
🏅4/10

🍺 Market Porter
🏢 Thornbridge
📝 One of the best porters I've had. Strong coffee flavour which didn't ruin the taste. Easily sat and drank this one (normally have to slog my way through a porter) and was slightly disappointed when I finished it. Good work @thornbridge

#porter #beerreview #craftbeer #drinking #hailtotheale #aleporn #alereview #bottledbeer #porterthoughts #beersofinstagram #beertography

🍺 Pilsner
🏢 Seven Brothers
📝 Sorry for all the new beer spam, @beer52hq delivered this week. This, hands down, is one of the best lagers (pilsners) I've ever had and would easily pick it over many of the ales I have previously drunk. #craftbeer #beerstagram #ale #alereview #alereviewer #beergasm #beersofinstagram #beer #pilsner #lagerlife

🍺 Black
🏢 Boss Brewing
📝 This "seriously silky stout" was anything but. Had a bit of an odd fizz to it and was not a scratch on the porter from the other night. The bottle design also leaves a lot to be desired. Overall, not that impressed.
🏅3/10

🍺 Monument Valley

📝 First beer out of the @flavourlyhq box from this week is a @fourpure classic. 🍺 Dark Skies
🏢 Drygate Brewing Company
📝 This is an odd one and not a usual stout from @drygate (via @flavourlyhq). There was no initial shiver you get from a full-on flavoured stout and it wasn't the usual heavy taste. Somewhere there was a hint of coffee, but nothing overpowering. A good gentle sipper, which could be mixed in with a session, rather than being a nightcap

🍺 Salzburger
🏢 Steigl
📝A "tasty" lager. Good depth and flavour but wouldn't actively buy again!
🏅5/10

🍺 New Zealand Ultra Pale
🏢 First Chop Brewing Arm
📝 Such a non-descript beer. Reminded me a lot of the Octopus Energy craft lager, just slightly worse. It was so bland I'm struggling to write anything about it. Yet to find a @flavourlyhq beer which is top drawer
🏅4/10

🍺 Revival
🏢 Moor Beer Company
📝 This beer from @drinkmoorbeer was in my latest @flavourlyhq box. There were 4 of them in the box and after 3, I'm still not sure what to make of it. It tastes like you've just eaten a sweet and had a swig of beer (sometimes), watery (sometimes) or like a fine craft beer (sometimes). I would drink again, but re-buyability is low.
🏅5/10

🍺 Birds and Bees
🏢 Williams Brothers Brewing Company
📝 Another "meh" beer from Flavourly. However tempting, I must never buy from them again. It's just a box of bland beers. 
🏅 5/10

🍺 Camden Hells
🏢 Camden Town Brewery
📝 Not sure if this strictly belongs on an Ale review account, although this has now turned into a "beer in a vessel" kind of place. Rescued this from the desolate office the other week as it was due to go out of date soon. This lager was nice and I would happily drink again. Preferably in a garden or other outdoors place with it being chilled. Nice hoppy taste, easy sippin' and went down well.

#beerthoughts #beersofinstagram #bottledbeer #beerporn #craftbeer #beerweekend #thirstythursday #beer #review #beerstagram

🍺 Eternal
🏢 Northern Monk
📝 Another absolute corker from the @northernmonk team. Smashing it out of the park. Once my fridge is bordering on empty again I'll order some more - with a matching glass too. Fantastic flavour and horrifically moreish. Shame it was my last one!
🏅9/10

🍺 The Shy Retirer
🏢 Bad Co.

🍺 USA
🏢 First Chop Brewing Arm
📝 Getting a bit bored now of writing about mediocre beers from mediocre beer subscription services. I won't be buying from Flavourly again as there wasn't one beer I would eagerly buy again. This was the same, it was "nice" but there are plenty of other IPAs out there that absolutely smash this. 
🏅6/10

🍺 Slow Burn
🏢 Polly's
📝 I feel like I was deceived with this beer as nowhere on the can does it say it was a sour. I only found out when I googled and discovered it was a tweak from a previous sour brew. It tasted like fizzy orange and lemon squash. Managed to finish the whole drink (maybe egged on by the novelty of drinking out a new glass) but I definitely won't be buying this again. 
🏅 3/10

🍺 Said & Done
🏢 Track
📝 I've seen plenty of @trackbrewingco beers floating around my timeline so I was excited to finally give one a go. Light, fruity, smooth and easy drinking. Certainly looking forward to trying more of their beers. 
🏅9/10
#thirstythursday #beerporn #drinking #beergasm #beer #craftale #alereview #pint #craftbeer #aleporn

🍺 Rock the Bit
🏢 Polly's
📝 This is the beer I was hoping the other one would be. Smooth, full of flavour and easy drinking. Could sink a few of these of an evening. Went down well with a game of Catan
🏅8/10

🍺 Striding Edge
🏢 Northern Monk
📝 My last of the @northernmonk beers from Mrs. Ale House. Didn't expect much from this 2.8% as the low ABV beers tend to lack some body. A tasty tipple, but nowhere near the absolute bangers they have otherwise produced. Takes a while to get into, but eventually becomes moreish. 
🏅7/10

🍺 King Slayer
🏢 Buxton Brewery
📝 After a months hiatus from posting new beers (don't worry, I was still drinking beer - I just fancied a few weeks of drinking some old favourites without having to think of scores), I thought I would get back on the wagon and post this beauty. I've had a few @buxtonbrewery beers in the past and some have been a bit meh, but this one is incredible. Smooth, very drinkable and a drinking 8%. Reminded me of a couple of Fourpure beers. Excellent stuff Buxton!

#hailtotheale #beerfluencer #review #beer #beerthoughts #thirstythursday #pint #beerweekend #aleporn

🍺 New Dawn
🏢 Shepherd Neame
📝 Good to be back on the bottled beers again after a stint of cans. Drank last week but I can still remember the fruity lightness this beer carried. Plenty of carbonation, so more of a summer tipple, but pleasingly tasty
🏅8/10

🍺 New England IPA
🏢 Brewdog Vs. 
📝 Had this been 6 months ago, I wouldn't have been seen dead with a craft beer in my fridge. Lockdown, however, seems to have altered my perspective on such beer. This collaboration between @brewdog and @cloudwaterbrew is an absolute corker. Unexpected sediment at the end meant I couldn't quite get the last mouthful I was expecting, but good nonetheless. Perfect accompaniment to Tony Hawk's 1 & 2 on PS4
🏅 8/10

🍺 Brut IPA
🏢 10 4 Brewing
📝 An interesting beer from Aldi. Very much trying to jump onto the hipster beer bandwagon and they do a pretty good job at holding on. Good flavours and easy drinking, just not as morish as the big boys. Will be keeping my eye out if ever I find myself there again. 
🏅 7/10

🍺 Golden Ale
🏢 Harper's Brewing Company
📝 I was starting to think I should just post all the beers I buy from Aldi as a 7 and be done with it, but this one bucked the trend. I've never had a beer that started off bad and got worse with each sip. An ambience which was remeniscent of the CBD beer I had before. Managed to choke down the last few mouthfuls. This is definitely not a beer I will be buying again. 
🏅 3/10

🍺 Perpendicular
🏢 Briarbank Brewery
📝 A holiday away in Suffolk calls for a sample of the local beers. Drove to, and waited outside, the @briarbankbrewery bar to open - only to find on their website they are closed at the moment. With a heavy heart we headed back to the car. On the way, we stumbled upon @hopstersipswich which fortunately stocked the beer (plus a few others coming soon). This beer is a classic British bitter. Was lovely to taste this bottle conditioned golden goodness. Worth waiting for
🏅8.5/10

🍺 Whitstable Bay Organic Ale
🏢 Shepherd Neame
📝 Although the bottle says "Faversham Steam Brewery", I've tagged this under Shepherd Neame as they are known for the Whitstable Bay beers. Not as good as the original, a slight post-sip tang which I'm not too sure of. Picked this one up in co-op pre holiday (not knowing I would buy so much beer!)
🏅6/10

🍺 Double Nelson Fog
🏢 Burnt Mill Brewery
📝 Another Suffolk beer from holiday, this stonking double IPA was spot on for an evening sip. I've become a big fan of double IPAs after my tickle with Fourpure and this one from @burntmillbrewery ticks every box. 
🏅 9/10

🍺 ALD IPA
🏢 Brewdog
📝 There is an excellent story behind this beer, you should go and look it up - I've had my eye on it since it was released and I was very glad a long time friend of Ale House Rock had a can spare for me to try. It's a shame the taste didn't quite live up to the legend. I suppose it isn't that much different from Punk IPA, so if that's your jam then maybe you'll like it. 
🏅 6/10

🍺 Strange Seas
🏢 Adnams
📝 This collaboration with Beavertown was plain. A sub-par IPA really - I expected a better beer from these two breweries. Like a combo DVD/Video player - It doesn't make it twice as good.
🏅4/10

🍺 Mosaic Pale Ale
🏢 Adnams
📝 Another disappointing can from Adams, picked up while in their hometown of Southwold a couple of weeks ago. Once again, a forgettable taste which is such a shame. A smidge better than the last.
🏅5/10

🍺 New England IPA
🏢 Adnams
📝 I was starting to give up on Adams canned beer, but then this beauty came along. Crisp, full of flavour and lovely a pleasure to drink. Was disappointed when this one ended! Last of the Adams small cans and a cracker to finish on. 
🏅9/10

🍺 Great Glacial Lake
🏢 Burnt Mill Brewery
📝 I've yet to find a bad DIPA. Still making my way through the Suffolk beers, this beauty from the same brewery of the DIPA I had the other day (@burntmillbrewery) ticks every box. Might have to see if they deliver down south!
🏅8/10

🍺 Chuggernaut
🏢 Amundsen Brewery
📝 Hazy session IPA cracked open to put a drop in the Toad in the Hole this evening. Nothing special, tasted like I should be in a trendybar with some "bros" talking loudly nearby
🏅6/10

🍺 Briar Pilsner
🏢 Briarbank Brewery
📝 A lovely full bodied, crisp pilsner which went well with the homemade Mexican meal yesterday. Wished I'd picked up a few more beers from @briarbankbrewery when I was in Suffolk. 
🏅 8/10

🍺 Gose to Hollywood
🏢 To Øl
📝 Mrs. Ale House picked me this up in M&S and what a disappointment. It reminds me of the "Tart" I had a couple of years ago. It tastes like a cheap orange Fanta knock-off. Had to go down the drain but, as with the tart, it gets a bonus point for Mrs. Ale House liking it
🏅 2/10

🍺 Kobolo
🏢 Adnams
📝 Last of the Suffolk beers, this @adnams beer is one of taste. Opened after the disaster of the previous beer, this lager (yes lager) was a refreshing change. Light, fizzy and flavoursome

🍺 Faked Alaska
🏢 Arbor

It was an odd taste to begin with (and, unsurprisingly didn't go with my dinner) but once I got into it I started to really enjoy it. Wouldn't rush for it again, but was certainly as experience I'm glad I had. Had a bit of yeast in the can, which I wasn't expecting

🍺 The Hop Stepper
🏢 The Hop Foundary
📝 Finally, an Aldi beer I can get on board with. Darker than I was expecting, but a lovely refreshing beer not too hoppy at all. I'll be picking a few of these up next time I'm in there.

🍺 India Pale Lager
🏢 10 4 Brewing

🍺 Anti Establishment IPA
🏢 Aldi
📝 Well, this one caught me by surprise. The catalyst for the Ald IPA I reviewed a few weeks ago, I was not expecting great things from this, based on the fact I am neither a fan of Pink IPA nor their IPA love-child. However, this was darn tasty. Crisp, flavoursome and a cut above the others. This is want Punk should taste like and I will definitely be hunting more of these down

🍺 Golden Torch
🏢 Williams Brothers Brewing Company
📝 A classic disappointing Aldi beer. Flavourless and bland. 
🏅 4/10

🍺 Pale Ale
🏢 Wolfox
📝 Picked up this at the train station on Friday after a week in the office. Wasn't expecting much from @wolfoxcoffee (being, what looks like, primarily coffee roasters) but this little gem was tasty. Crisp with a clean finish. Perfect as a train or desk beer I can imagine, but also pretty good with a pizza.
🏅8/10

🍺 Amber Ale
🏢 Harper's Brewing Company
📝 An attempt at a classic beer from Harper's (Aldi's own brewery). It's got the body of a bitter, but the after taste of an Aldi beer.

🍺 Double Punk
🏢 Brewdog
📝 After seeing this double IPA on my feed, I felt like I had to try it. I've been a big fan of DIPAs recently, but this is the worst one yet (however, is it still better than a normal Punk IPA). Over 8%, and I felt it, there was an unusual post sip-twang I want keen on. I would still definitely have another one of these if offered.

🍺 Hazy Jane
🏢 Brewdog
📝 I had started to give up on @brewdogofficial as anything more than an "ok" brewery, but these last two beers are starting to make me come round to them. Maybe it's just their mainstream "available everywhere" beers are their most average. This was a corker and I could drink this for many hours.

🍺 Olympia
🏢 Harvey's Brewery
📝 Picked this up thinking it was an old faithful I'd reviewed many moons ago, but a quick check of the index (link in bio) revealed this one was missing. I've had many a pint of this and it never fails to tick the boxes. Not an outstanding beer, but I've never had a bad pint. Cheers @harveysbrewery!

🍺 Spin Drift
🏢 Gun Brewery
📝 My first foray into @gunbrewery beers and a good one to start. Full of flavour and a nice light IPA to kick things off, this went down very well for Christmas Eve. Although as 7, this could very easily be an 8 if I was in the right mood or it was sunny and warm!
🏅 7/10

🍺 Scaramanga

🍺 Numb Angel
🏢 Gun Brewery

🍺 Parabellum
🏢 Gun Brewery

🍺 Atlantic Pale Ale

🍺 Brighton Tea Party

🍺 Red IPA
🏢 Gun Brewery

🏅7/10

🍺 Far Fetched
🏢 Hairy Dog Brewery
📝 An enjoyable IPA. Can't say much about it, good or bad, but I generally enjoyed it. 
🏅 7/10

🍺 Prospect
🏢 Hepworth & Co Brewers 
📝 It's good to have a pint of bitter again. Got a bit shaken up in a backpack walking home but was an excellent beer nonetheless. Hepworth are excellent local brewers - I need to hunt some more down

🍺 Neck Oil
🏢 Beavertown

🍺 How's the Serenity?

📝 Beer one of the zoom tasting evening with @laurzstreet. We both ordered the January mystery mix box from @beermerchants. Started with this one which has the most dubious description (to be served with Apple and Vinaigrette pudding?). This instantly took me back to a summer evening on Brighton beach. 🏢 Singlecut

🏅7/10

🍺 Kölsch
🏢 Früh
📝 Very lager-like. Light and refreshing after the slightly heavier IIPA before. Managed to drink this one pretty quickly, so can't have been too offensive. Apparently true Kölsch beers are like champagne in that it can only get the name if it is brewed in the region of Cologne
🏅6/10

🍺 Bloodhound
🏢 Hairy Dog Brewery
📝 A massively tasteful red IPA. Packed with flavour and an incredibly smooth drink. Would definitely drink again
🏅 9/10

🍺 Lucky Jack
🏢 Lervig Aktiebryggeri
📝 A simple IPA which neither offends nor compliments the palate. Easy drinking, without a desire to drink more. Could a beer be any more mediocre?
🏅5/10

🍺 Saison Van Du Bruwer
🏢 De la Senne
📝 A subtle lemony summer drink which is going down quite well after a Sunday roast. Would be better on a warm evening in the garden, but in the lounge will suffice. A Saison, I feel, needs to be drunk from an "elegant" glass and this @maltusfaber one is one of my faves. 
🏅 7/10

🍺 Life in the clouds
🏢 Collective Arts
📝 A double dry hopped IPA to see in my Thursday evening. First sip was a bit of a disappointment but as it went on, it got a lot better - so much so I didn't want to finish it! There is a bit of a strong hoppy aftertaste, which knocks it down a point but still a pretty solid pint.

🍺 Whitbier
🏢 Wolfox
📝 While Wolfox do excellent coffee, this beer left a lot to be desired. A bit too clear for a wheat beer and had a zingy aftertaste. Not to my liking, but went down all the same. 
🏅3/10

🍺 Shipping out of Boston
🏢 Jack's Abbey
📝 A lager that's this colour?! And a lager that tastes good?! Wonderfully light and lager-like with a decent flavour to boot. Despite the good review, it is still a lager and still has lager qualities, so can't get max marks from me. If this was a lager review account instead though, then well, I'd have bigger problems to worry about (like why I'm running a lager review account 😉).

#lager #lagerreview #lagerbeer #lagerdrink #lagerthoughts #lagerporn #lagerlife #lagerreviewer #beerreview #beerinaglass

🍺 Schlenkerla Marzen
🏢 Heller
📝 The first bite (and yes it was a bite, not a sip), my mouth was assailed with the taste of smokey meat and a light porter. It was reminiscent of an Italian antipasti - some peppery/smoked thin slices of sausage served with a complex beer all within a glass. After a while, the novelty wore off and the last third was a drag to finish (would have been better as a 330ml). It also, oddly, made my tongue feel a bit numb. Definitely wouldn't have it again, gets a bonus point for unusualness.

🍺 Scafell
🏢 Northern Monk
📝 Home. A @northernmonk Beer in a Northern Monk class. Had to keep reminding myself not to chug this 6.5% IPA, but it was tricky as it was so damn tasty. 🍺 Triple Hazy
🏢 Brewdog
📝 The triple hopped version of the Hazy Jane I had a couple of weeks ago. This triple IPA packs an absolute punch at 9.5%. Certainly not one to be chugged but after a monotonous Friday, it was a great end. Felt lightheaded after a third but had a great taste with no chemical after-burn which you might expect from a beer of this ABV.  It's a great tasting beer but don't think I would queue them up very often - maybe once in a while as a treat. There is a Double Hazy, which might be a perfect combo.

🍺 IPA
🏢 Wolfox
📝 I actually bought one of these when I picked up the Pale Ale of theirs back in December, but completely forgot to post it. Of course, it meant I had to buy another one so I could remember what it was like. Light, fruity, refreshing and very easily drinkable. Smashing work @wolfoxcoffee (also meant I could buy some coffee when ordering, too)
🏅8/10

🍺 London Pride
🏢 Fuller's
📝 I realised two things this week. First, my feed has become very craft-beery. This is mainly because of the readily available craft tipples in lockdown - it seems they swiftly adapted to postal pints. This isn't very good for an "Ale" review Instagram. The second thing I've realised was that I have never reviewed a lot of classic beers, like this one. Tasty, easy drinking, never disappointing
🏅 7/10
#beerreview #alereview #fullers #londonpride #ale #beer @fullers

🍺 Saucery
🏢 Magic Rock Brewing
📝 This beer was an IPA and just that. It was drinkable (it didn't make me turn my nose up). I wouldn't rush out and buy it again, but if I was round someone's house (ha, I wish) I would not turn one down. 
🏅 6/10

🍺 Proper Job
🏢 St. Austell Brewery
📝 Another absolute banger of a classic I've not reviewed before. A crisp, golden ale and a great bitter. They've done a proper job with this one. 
🏅8/10

🍺 Dirty Arthur
🏢 Riverside Brewery
📝 Beer delivery day means beer on a Tuesday. This one tastes exactly how you imagine a beer festival to taste. Slightly cloudy (putting that down to not having time to settle) but an enjoyable pint nonetheless. Taking a sip of this transports you to days of yonder, where you would choose an ale from a stained leaflet purely based on the name, stagger up to beer belly tubbed man and ask for some in your glass. Not quite suited to home drinking, but a good nostalgia trip. @riverside_brewery
🏅6/10

🍺 Beeding Best
🏢 Riverside Brewery
📝 Second beer from @riverside_brewery and one that is a lot clearer, and comes with a good solid taste. I think it is taking my taste-buds a bit of time to adjust from the smooth hipster craft beers to the rugged ales. Ordering from Riverside is certainly a comforting taste excursion; taking me back to "old man" pubs with friends, sitting by open fires in the winter. Lovely flavours and easy to drink. A bit of a bitter tang after each sip.
🏅7/10

🍺 Sneaky Steamer
🏢 Riverside Brewery
📝 A bit too grapefruity for me, I finished the drink but wouldn't rush to have another one of these. Despite that, I can't say enough how much these beers belong in a rustic pub or at a beer festival and how much I'm enjoying the comfort. 
🏅 4/10

🍺 Rambling Monarch
🏢 Riverside Brewery
📝 Had this the other day and it was the best one so far. The second one I had the following day was not so good, so basin g my review on the first. Fresh, bitter and a good ale.
🏅 8/10

🍺 Old Peculiar
🏢 Theakston
📝 A lovely full bodied old ale which has a hint of sweetness. Was a perfect way of ending an evening and hit the spot as a post-dinner tipple. This was another beer that I've had countless times but never reviewed!
🏅 8/10

🍺 Tangmere Tower
🏢 Riverside Brewery
📝 This was another one I had two of, both tasting wildly different. The first one was a 4 or 5, but I enjoyed this one a lot more. Made me feel like I was having my nachos in a country pub. A classic ale
🏅 7/10

🍺 Love Bug
🏢 Cellar Head & Only With Love
 📝 What a fantastic beer. Darker than I was expecting but full of flavour. Thoroughly enjoyed this and was disappointed when I finished. Don't often award half points but this gets one 👍. Great work @cellarheadbrew and @onlywith_love and shout out to @palate.bottleshop for stocking. 
🏅8.5/10

🍺 Honeycomb Milkshake
🏢 Unbarred x Bison Beer
📝How the hell did they make this beer taste like a milkshake?! The first few sips threw me right into a 'shake flashback. Once my taste-buds had settled, this wonderfully easy-drinking beer ticked a lot of my boxes. Despite being great tasting, I wouldn't add it to my regular "buy again" list. 🍺 Locals

🍺 Tubbers' Tipple
🏢 Riverside Brewery
📝 Another good beer from @riverside_brewery. Lovely tasting ale, only slightly let down by the slight after taste of liquorice, which is not to my personal liking (to which it loses a half point). Looking forward to the second bottle of this I've still got in the fridge

🏢 Riverside Brewery
📝 I feel like this review is more for Mead in general than specifically this one. I've never had mead before so thought I would take the opportunity when I ordered my recent batch of beers from @riverside_brewery. It's very fizzy, very sweet and very light. Would happily have it as a prosecco/champagne/toast alternative drink at an occasion, but not one for drinking at home in front of the TV really. Reminded me of Appletise.

A bit too much of a "salt" taste (I know it's salted caramel) for my liking but still pleasurable.

🏢 Riverside Brewery

🍺 Arise
🏢 Burning Sky
📝 When I went into @palate.bottleshop a couple of weeks ago, I asked for the owners favourite beer. After a minutes pause she rattled off plenty of options and settled on Arise, saying it was one of their most popular beers and always sold out. I can see why it's popular, and it reminded me a lot of Hophead by Dark Star. It's a bit too much of a hoppy beer for me. I can tell this is just personal taste and if I was into light hoppy, pilsner beers it would be top dog.

🍺 Summer Pale Ale
🏢 Co-Op
📝 Yes, I know it isn't summer yet but in our local Co-op you have to queue past the beer so I picked this up on a whim. Light and easy drinking, would be good in a garden on a hot day.
🏅 7/10

🍺 Levy
🏢 McEwan's
📝 Had this sitting in my fridge for a while as I was reluctant to drink it. I've had it a few times before and always been unimpressed. It's trying to be a black IPA (or a "light" stout) but falls short of the mark. There are some coffee/chocolate notes there but they get overwhelmed by the watery taste. 
🏅 4/10

🍺 Bishops Finger
🏢 Shepherd Neame
📝 I can't believe I'm 448 beers and over 4 years into this review malarkey and I've only just got round to posting a Bishops Finger. No, it's not from some 2 person hipster garage and yes, it is available in every kind of shop with an alcohol license. But, as I was drinking this this evening, I was reminded about how good a beer it is. Full bodied, morish and a good old classic ale. I'd not bought one in a while as, for some reason, my memory of it was not favourable, but it's a very tasty solid pint. 
🏅 9/10

A 4 pack of their Lost Lager finally rocked up yesterday, so I proper battle tested it this evening with 2 cans. Not really an evening sipping beer, but certainly did the job. 🍺 Stars and Stripes
🏢 Northern Monk

#beer #stout @northernmonk #stoutreview #stoutinacan #beersofinstagram #beerstagram

🍺 Lock Down
🏢 Brewdog
📝 An interesting beer from @brewdogofficial. Thought I would go straight out the gates from the Lockdown Survival Kit with this Lock Down beer (their inconsistent spelling of Lockdown irks me). This "cream soda pilsner" is definitely a novelty drink. It's a "I drank it for the experience" kind of drink. I definitely wouldn't buy it again and I would probably say no if offered one. It tastes very much like cream soda, took me back to my childhood, with an alcoholic twist. Would recommend for the nostalgia but that's about it. 
🏅4/10

🍺 Fake Empire
🏢 Brewdog
📝 This beer was as disappointing as I was expecting. I don't tend to buy "sour" beers but this came in the Lockdown kit from Brewdog, so I had to try it. First taste is watery, followed by the sour hit. After swallowing, you get a hint of a "cool" breath - much like an Airwaves chewing gum. It got better as it went on but I still couldn't finish it. This beer did, however, confirm that my non-drinking wife does find sour beers "ok".
🏅2/10

🍺 Model Citizen
🏢 Brewdog Vs.
📝 @brewdog Vs @saltbrewing - this tasty NEIPA went down well with my pizza. Slightly thicker than your average IPA which made it a slow sipper - which is just what I was after. Nice taste too
🏅 7/10

🍺 Layer cake
🏢 Brewdog
📝 This is a dessert stout if ever I had one. Marshmallow and chocolate and you can taste it all. Reminds me of a beer flavoured marshmallow rather than the other way round. A thick stout which is definitely best enjoyed over an evening. Probably not with a big heavy dinner as it's going to fill you up. You can't really taste the 7% kick until you go to standup. I did enjoy it though, went very well with film night
🏅 7/10

🍺 Twice Tangled
🏢 Badger
📝 With my recent frivolity into Double IPAs, this somewhat piqued my interest when I saw it on the Tesco website. Not only is it going back to my traditional beer-tasting routes with a classic ale, it has the modern twist of my recent flirtations. I've had this double hopped version of Badger's Tanglefoot in the fridge for a couple of weeks - was saving it as an accompaniment to a roast dinner (to which it was a great match). There was some of the DIPA characteristics there, but not enough for my liking. It was more of an intense flavour of a beer which didn't need it. Nice enough, but I think I'll stick to the classic Tanglefoot if I'm feeling that way inclined. 
🏅 6/10
#beer #ale #dipa

🍺 Anchor Steam Beer
🏢 Anchor Brewing
📝 A brew from 1896 (I assume the recipe, not the beer), this random beer was picked up in Asda last week. Crisp and inoffensive, it was the kind of beer you would expect to find in a Gastro pub or wine bar, when they want some "cool" token beers in. It had some flavour, but nothing to shout about. 
🏅 5/10
#beer #bottledbeer #ale

🍺 Mallow Laser Quest
🏢 Brewdog
📝 This was an unusual one to get used to and not really one to have with dinner (more of a post-dinner beer, really). A consistency that reminded me of my pineapple juice and lemonade days (with no fizz) combined with a slight after-taste of the mallow. Very sweet and the first beer (in over 450) that both my wife and I liked. Can't say I would buy this again, but enjoyed it nonetheless. 
🏅 7/10

🍺 Duo-polis
🏢 Brewdog
📝 Maybe it was a mistake have this one straight after the Mallow quest, but this beer was disappointing. I was hoping for a thicker beer (as it was an oat cream) but turned out to be a fizzy IPA with not much flavour. 
🏅 4/10

🍺 Beach Break
🏢 Harbour
📝 This was an odd one. I was expecting an old fashioned ale, but a pale ale with a lemon twist. Took me a few sips to get into it, but once I found the groove I was able to enjoy it. Definitely wouldn't have it again, but was palatable for the evening. 
🏅 5/10

🍺 Double Hazy
🏢 Brewdog
📝 I've finally completed the triple. Finished the Hazy series. The double hopped version of @brewdogofficial's Hazy Jane (and the little brother of Triple Hazy), this beer has the best of both worlds. The kick that comes with a DIPA without the overbearing of alcohol found in the triple. This beer got better the more I drank it, and the 7.4% was a curve ball that snuck up on me. Would highly recommend trying the original first then moving onto this. Killer beer. 
🏅 9/10

🍺 Jack Hammer
🏢 Brewdog
📝 Another day, another Brewdog. This beer is described on the can as a "ruthless IPA" and, while I wouldn't go so far as to call it ruthless, it was certainly more abrasive than the other IPAs from @brewdogofficial. A welcome change from the sea of pale ales in the Lock Down Survival Kit, this 7.2% beer was one that got better the more you drank it. I can almost say I _really_ enjoyed it by the time I got to the last sip.

🍺 Juice Shack
🏢 Brewdog

I'm still not sure. I drank the whole can, I didn't turn my nose up at all but I didn't crave for more once I'd finished. A bit like the "Cream Soda Pilsner" I had the other week, it seems like a novelty. I'm glad I experienced it, but I wouldn't do it again anytime soon.

🍺 Lock Down
🏢 Brewdog
📝 I've come to the conclusion that Brewdog should stick to brewing "normal beers". Their fruity and alternative beers are novel at best and just plain old disappointing most of the time. 🍺 Barnard Castle Eye Test
🏢 Brewdog
📝 Now this is what @brewdogofficial are good at. Smooth and tasty hazy IPAs with no gimmicks. No weird flavours or curveball antics this beer had a lovely taste that was wonderfully morish.

#brewdog #barnardcastle #ipa #hazyipa #easter #beer #beergasm #drinking #bottle #beerporn #ale #beerstagram #beerweekend #beertography

🍺 Northern Star
🏢 Northern Monk
📝 This porter was slightly disappointing and quite forgettable. It was too sweet to be a classic chocolate and/or coffee porter of breweries old, but not sweet enough to be a dessert beer like the recent Brewdog one. Drinkable, but I won't be going back for another one.

🍺 Anytime IPA
🏢 Meantime Brewing Company
📝 Instead of chocolate this year, Ma and Pa bought me beer for Easter. 🍺 Jet Stream
🏢 Brewdog
 📝 The final beer from my Brewdog Lockdown Survival Kit and what a disappointment to end on. A "transatlantic pale ale" which means it's gone around the world a d lost its flavour. It tasted like a poor, mass-produced lager. 
🏅2/10

🍺 Divine Faith
🏢 Northern Monk
📝 I'm a huge fan of the original Faith and have come to love DIPAs of late, so when I saw @northernmonk release a DIPA of Faith I had to order it (side note, very impressed with Northern Monk's delivery service once again). This beer had the colour and consistency of apple juice, which was quite confusing at first. It's a thick, full beer which is not to be drunk quickly and lasted all evening. Full of flavour and a great end to the day. It's not as good as the Faith, but still a very good DIPA 
🏅8/10

🍺 Lupuoid
🏢 Beavertown
📝 A tasty IPA from @beavertownbeer picked up from Sainsbury's. Easy drinking with plenty of flavour - would happily add this to my rotation. 
🏅8/10

🍺 Huddle
🏢 Gipsy Hill
📝A lovely IPA from @gipsyhillbrew, given to me by @laurzstreet. Super tasty and easy drinking. Gipsy Hill consistently do excellent drinks and this is no exception. 
🏅8/10

🍺 Q.E.D Session Pale
🏢 London Beer Factory 
📝 I wasn't much of a fan of this one. Reminded me very much of a lager. I didn't think there was much taste there. It improved when my curry turned up as it complimented it well, but it was missing something. 
🏅4/10

🍺 Don't Mess with Yorkshire
🏢 Northern Monk
📝 A roast dinner calls for a real ale, a bitter, and old man beer. This is exactly what accompanied my Bank Holiday Monday roast - @northernmonk's answer to the ale. It tasted like a good ale and certainly worked well with the roast potatoes. It had good body and colour and I would certainly drink it again.
🏅7/10

🍺 DIPA
🏢 Unbarred
📝 After the last few @unbarredbrewery beers I was excited about this DIPA offering. Unfortunately, it didn't live up to my expectations. I know this is an odd comment for an 8% beer, but you could taste too much of the alcohol. Strong and unforgiving - I think I'll leave this one on the shelf next time. 
🏅 4/10

🍺 Diceman
🏢 Wychwood Brewery
📝 How can a brewery which gets IPAs and, in fact, every other type of beer so right get a stout so wrong. I could just about taste the chocolate coffee notes that are so often found in a stout below the slightly watery flavour. Didn't really enjoy this beer at all, which is a shame really. I did manage to finish it though. 
🏅3/10

🍺 For Your Eyes Only
🏢 Northern Monk
📝 This was a tasty DDH IPA which went down without incident. Nothing groundbreaking but a pleasurable drink.
🏅7/10

🍺 Parade
🏢 Beak Brewery
📝 I've swiftly become a big fan of @thebeakbrewery beers (need to head over there for a pint soon) and this one was no exception. Picked up from @palate.bottleshop a couple of weeks back, this IPA was smooth and fruity and very easy drinking. Had to pace myself as I found I was drinking it extremely quickly...
🏅8/10

🍺 Written in the Dust
🏢 Deya Brewing Company
📝 Since my first drink of theirs, I've always held @deyabrewery with high regard. Looking back, however, I realise I have only ever had one or their beers. However, my trust was not misguided as this was another good one 👌. It was a lush, thick creamy DIPA. Perfect for an evening in (I could imagine it being quite filling if you decided to have a second). 
🏅 8/10

🍺 Ranger
🏢 Gipsy Hill 
📝 A light inoffensive IPA. Easy enough to drink but nothing I would rave about. Would drink again at a BBQ or similar. Picked up from Tesco. 🏅 6/10

🍺 Camden Pale
🏢 Camden Town Brewery
📝 A fairly plain, non-descript pale
🏅 5/10

🍺 Ikat
🏢 Salt Beer Factory
📝 My first foray into @saltbeerfactory (having heard plenty of good things). This very juicy DDH IPA was nice enough, but nothing I would buy again. Kind of left a dry taste in my mouth but pleasant nonetheless.
🏅 6/10

🍺 Zia
🏢 London Beer Factory
📝 What a tough beer to rate. A 9.5% Tiramisu Pastry stout which was as sickly as it sounds. 440ml is too much of this beer - even 330ml is too big. First few sips of this were promising; you could smell the coffee without getting too close. The flavours were sweet and the kind of thing I was after yesterday evening after a meal out. I opened this at 8pm, and by 10:30pm I had only made it two thirds of the way through the glass - with some still left in the can. Very heavy, very thick and very sweet. Picked up at Tesco
🏅 3/10

🍺 18.05 Sandy Kerr // North Sea Sessions
🏢 Northern Monk
📝 An IPA/Lager fushion (IPL), I wasn't hugely impressed with this Northern Monk beer brewed with Donzoko Brewing Company. It tasted like a plain hoppy lager. Not for me
🏅4/10

🍺 Order of the Faith
🏢 Northern Monk
📝 A collaboration between @northernmonk and @alphadeltabeerand part of the "Twist on Faith" series. A classic hazy DIPA which was fruity and smooth, easy drinking and a delight.
🏅 8/10

🍺 Session IPA
🏢 Toast
📝 I don't normally go for "Session IPAs" as can be a bit bland. This ale from @toastale, however, was full of flavour and delightfully morish. It was a shame to see the end of it. Toast Ales are made from surplus bread and all their profits go to charity - so not only did this beer taste great, I felt morally better by drinking it. A little present from my mum
🏅 9/10

🍺 Iskold Classic
🏢 Mikkeller
📝 This was not the colour I was expecting this "lager" to be. Decent taste and certainly drinkable, just nothing outstanding
🏅 6/10

🍺 Mr. President
🏢 Brewdog
📝 I was quite excited to see a small @brewdog DIPA as it seemed to tick all my boxes, however the taste did not live up to the hype. First sip felt promising, but it was swiftly followed by a flat flavour with a slightly chemical aftertaste. Such a shame
🏅4/10

🍺 42|DDH Pale Ale
🏢 Brew by Numbers
📝 Crisp, light and fresh Pale Ale with the added pizzazz of being double dry hopped. This might be the taste of cold beer after a long hot day but this was damn refreshing. This little number was part of the @brewdogofficial and friends box I got for free as a trial. Smashing work @brewbynumbers 
🏅 8/10
#ddh #paleale #beer #bankholiday #doubledryhopped #beersunday #bankholidayfunday #beerstagram #beersofinstagram

🍺 Locals V2
🏢 Beak Brewery
📝 This is it, the 500th beer. Who would have thought that a 24-beer Ale advent calendar would turn into this? @thebeakbrewery are a brewery I have only recently discovered. Hailing from not-too-far Lewes, they brew consistently high quality beers - I wanted to be this that took the spot. 

I want to try all their creations (shout out to another recent discovery @palate.bottleshop for stocking them) and have a fair few lined up in the fridge. 

This is a second iteration of a beer I have previously rated at 9.5 (and coincidentally my celebration beer once my daughter was born). This one, like the previous, is so very easy drinking, fruity, thick and a damn good drink. 
🏅 9/10

🍺 Let's Go
🏢 Only With Love
📝 This IPA had a great first few sips when it was straight out the fridge but as the beer went on I fell slightly out of love with it. Can imagine it would be better suited drunk quickly and ice cold - not slowly over a warm evening (sorry I didn't do it justice). Only With Love are a small local brewery that seem to be popping up more and more. Will keep an eye out for more of their beers as this seemed promising but didn't quite work for me. 
🏅6/10

🍺 Bluebell
🏢 360 Degree Brewing Company
📝 A refreshing change from hipster (D/T)IPAs. A classic bitter from a Brighton-based micro brewery. A little watery but some great flavours hiding below the surface. Looking forward to trying some more from these brewers.
🏅 7/10

🍺 IPA
🏢 Unbarred
📝 A simple no frills IPA @unbarredbrewery. Not overly fruity (which is a lovely change), nice and smooth with still the classic IPA tang. Great to have a run-of-the-mill beer from Unbarred to see what they are really capable of. And it's good. 
🏅 8/10

🍺 Jungle Trip
🏢 London Beer Factory
📝 I didn't realise I was such a fan of New England IPAs until @marclittlemore asked for some recommendations and all the NEIPAs I had recently drunk scored highly. I grabbed this one when I saw it on the Tesco shelves and was not disappointed. It was smooth easy drinking with plenty of flavour and definitely one I will buy again. @londonbeerfactory
🏅 9/10
#neipa #ipa #beer #londonbeer #sunday #sundaybeer #coldbeer #coldbeers #beerstagram #beersofinstagram

🍺 Big Baller
🏢 Gipsy Hill
📝 Popped into the @craftbeercabin at the weekend to grab a park beer and this caught my eye as a take-home-and-try kind of beer. It was only when I got outside did I realise this TIPA from @gipsyhillbrew is 11.3%! Was a bit disappointed in how it poured - it was very sediment-y and this photo was taken after a while of letting it settle and pouring into a couple of glasses to try and reduce. This beer, for me, was very much a "try it" beer. The flavours were there (along with a very strong punch) but I won't be buying one again - no doubt marred by the pour
🏅 5/10

🍺 Into the Haze
🏢 Deya Brewing Company
📝 Another corker from @deyabrewery, fruity, refreshing and very easy drinking. Reminded me of cloudy apple juice in colour and texture but a light beer in taste. Could drink a few of these and fully appreciate the 500ml. Picked this IPA up from @palate.bottleshop a couple of weeks ago. Best served cold
🏅 8/10
#deya #ipa #holiday #beeroclock #beerstagram #beergasm #beerporn #ipareview #beer #beerstagrammers #beeryourself

🍺 Stur
🏢 Beak Brewery
📝 Another DIPA from @thebeakbrewery and another absolute banger. A bit more of a hoppy/harsh DIPA, not as smooth and hazy as the other ones I've had from them. The 8.5% snacks you in the face  with this one and leaves no prisoners. A perfect one-can-of-an-evening, kind of drink. Picked up at @palate.bottleshop - looks like I'll have to go back there soon too, to pick up more Beak beer!
🏅 8/10

🍺 American Pale Ale
🏢 Toast
📝 The final @toastale I got from my mum and would say it was my second favourite. Crisp, light and easy drinking. Nice way to finish off an afternoon of drinking in the pub and accompanied dinner wonderfully. Wouldn't go out of my way to hunt one of these down, but would pick one up if I was passing
🏅 7/10

🍺 Northern Mischief
🏢 Wooha
📝 A friend came round to play board games and bought a couple of @woohabrewing beers with him. This bottle conditioned lager was a crisp little number. Very lively (hence the big head) but light and refreshing. As with any lager, there was no notably "big" flavour. 
🏅 7/10

🍺 Alnwick IPA
🏢 The Alnwick Brewery Co. LTD
📝 Sorry for the lack off-brand photo, I'm visiting Mum and Dad Ale House after their holiday (and drinking their beer). Not very impressed with this one, had a wholesome smell but an odd aftertaste (and taste in general). Not to keen on this one
🏅 4/10

🍺 Farne Island
🏢 Hadrian Border Brewery
📝 A much cleaner, crisper beer and a pleasure to drink. Tasted like a good old fashioned IPA-style beer, although nothing spectacular. 
🏅 7/10
